item.title: US attacks deepen hardship, not dissent, in Iran's restive south
item.description:
By Parisa Hafezi and Angus McDowall

DUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Opposition to Iran's clerical rulers has long simmered in the country's impoverished south. But after bearing the brunt of U.S. strikes this month, many residents are directing their anger at President Donald Trump and an air campaign they say has upended their lives.

item.title: Iran's Araghchi reveals Turkey's role in thwarting Kurdish offensive
item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.

item.title: Iran's Araghchi reveals Turkey's role in thwarting Kurdish offensive
item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.

item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.

item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.

item.title: Israel to withdraw from Lebanon’s pilot zones ahead of Aoun-Trump meeting
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio Sunday during the first part of his four-day visit to Washington.

item.title: Israel to withdraw from Lebanon’s pilot zones ahead of Aoun-Trump meeting
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio Sunday during the first part of his four-day visit to Washington.

item.title: Israel to withdraw from Lebanon’s pilot zones ahead of Aoun-Trump meeting
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio Sunday during the first part of his four-day visit to Washington.

item.title: Israel to withdraw from Lebanon’s pilot zones ahead of Aoun-Trump meeting
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio Sunday during the first part of his four-day visit to Washington.

item.title: Oil transfer activity in Gulf of Oman slows following ship attacks, data shows
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul

LONDON, July 20 (Reuters) - Oil transfers between tankers in waters outside the Strait of Hormuz have slowed following a wave of recent attacks on vessels by Iranian forces, according to satellite analysis and sources.

Since the start of the Iran war in late February, ship-to-ship (STS) transfers between tankers have helped enable oil to leave the Gulf despite the threats to commercial vessels sailing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis declare naval blockade against Saudi Arabia
item.description:
(Fixes garble in paragraph 6)

By Nayera Abdallah and Timour Azhari

DUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, a move that threatens to escalate the conflict in the region and disrupt global energy supply and trade beyond the Gulf.

The Houthis' armed forces in a statement said they were declaring "a maritime embargo against the criminal Saudi enemy, based on the equation of 'an eye for an eye', effective immediately".

item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: Iran's president said his country is fighting a "full-scale war" with the United States, as Washington expanded its strikes across the Islamic republic and Tehran hit Gulf states. - Iran says in 'full-scale war' with US - Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said his country was in a "full-scale war" with the United States and that Tehran should "accept the natural consequences of this resistance".

item.title: Khalil al-Hayya: The Gaza negotiator who will now lead Hamas
item.description: For months, veteran Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya spearheaded the group's indirect ceasefire negotiations with arch-foe Israel in an effort to halt the war in Gaza. "Khalil al-Hayya was elected by an overwhelming majority," a senior Hamas official told AFP, adding that he is expected to formally assume the role next week.

item.title: Scars of war linger on Syria's Saint Simeon monastery
item.description: Near the base of a column atop which Saint Simeon Stylites once perched, scattered stones lay bare the damage inflicted on the surrounding Byzantine monastery in northern Syria by war, looting and rampant illegal excavations. The Saint Simeon monastery itself was subjected to "looting, illegal excavation, military use and neglect", according to site director Abdel Jalil Arabi.

item.title: Who is Hamas' new leader Khalil Al-Hayya?
item.description:
(Corrects Palestinian death toll to 73,000 from 67,000, in paragraph 17)

By Nidal al-Mughrabi

July 20 (Reuters) - Khalil Al-Hayya, a senior Hamas official who survived an Israeli raid on Qatar in September last year and who is seen as having good ties with Iran, was named on Monday as the overall leader of the Palestinian militant group.

Hayya, who lost a son in the Gaza conflict and two sons in previous wars, has been based with other Hamas leaders in Doha.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil tops $90 as Iran warns Hormuz 'will not be safe'
item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.description: Tensions escalated further after Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps vowed on Monday that not a "single drop" of oil or gas would pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Hamas names Khalil Al-Hayya as new overall leader
item.description:
By Nidal al-Mughrabi and Eman Abouhassira

CAIRO, July 20 (Reuters) - Hamas has named Khalil Al-Hayya as its overall leader, according to a statement from the Palestinian militant group on Monday, in a move an analyst says could signal a tougher stance by the group towards disarmament calls.

Hayya replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed in combat with Israeli forces in October 2024 as Hamas fought a full-scale, two-year war with Israel in the Gaza Strip.
